sanskrit:
यस्य नाहङ्कृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते।
हत्वापि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते॥
transliteration: yasya nāhaṅkṛto bhāvo buddhir yasya na lipyate | hatvāpi sa imāṃl lokān na hanti na nibadhyate ||
literal_meaning: Of whom there is no ego-made existence, whose intellect is not smeared—even having killed these worlds, he neither kills nor is bound.
contextual_meaning: When action is performed without egoic ownership and with an untainted, witnessing mind, the psychological karmic trace (guilt, self-recrimination) does not form; thus the actor remains inwardly free.
psychological_state: Flow-state of detached engagement: full presence to duty without internal narrative of being the 'doer' or 'sinner'.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon must amputate to save a life; if she identifies as 'I am causing harm' she freezes, but if she acts as an instrument of healing while maintaining compassionate witness, she avoids moral injury.
anchor_text: Act without ego or guilt; your mind stays free.
